name: Danny Kaye's Chinese Stir-Fry (Oysters and Shrimp)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound raw shrimp, shelled and deveined
2 tablespoons peanut, vegetable or corn oil
1 piece fresh ginger root, peeled and cut into fine shreds
5 scallions, trimmed and cut into 2-inch lengths
salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Place oysters in a mixing bowl; add flour and enough water to cover. Stir the oysters in the liquid.
2. Drain well, run under several changes of cold water, and drain well again. (The flour will cleanse and plump the oysters.)
3. Prepare shrimp by washing; set aside.
4. Drop the oysters into barely simmering water. Turn off heat. Let stand 1 minute; drain. Set aside.
5. Repeat with shrimp.
6. Heat oil in a wok or skillet over high heat. Add ginger and scallions. Cook, stirring for 5 seconds.
7. Add soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper to taste, stirring constantly.
8. Blend cornstarch into cold water; stir into the wok or skillet.
9. Cook for 10 seconds. Serve at once. Makes 8 servings.
